summary: Full UI test of the Address Approval Dashboard prototype (frontend-only). All 8 required flows verified: page loads with title/banner/metrics/10-row table; Fact Check popover shows 91%, 340/6,200, and Serviceable zone check 71%; Downstream Impact popover shows 0.6%, 38/6200, Trip feedback 12, Support tickets 14; Track Record popover shows Volume handled/day and Reject rate charts; only one popover open at a time; Emma Carter (100511) side panel shows Rejected badge, decided 8:14, requested May 15, Distance from prior address FAIL with 9.4/2.0/7.4 km + Haversine + Agent verdict '2 of 6 checks failed'; Lucas Turner (100507) panel shows Approved with 6 PASS / 0 FAIL; details panel closes via both close button and backdrop.
critical_code_review_comments: Popover switching UX: clicking a different metric card while a popover is open first hits the invisible full-viewport backdrop (z-30) which closes the current popover but does NOT open the newly-clicked card. Users must click the new card twice (or close via X, then click). Consider stopping propagation on metric cards or excluding the anchor card region from the backdrop so a single click switches popovers seamlessly. Currently mitigated in test spec by closing first, but this is friction in real use.
seed_data_creation: None. All data is hardcoded in /app/frontend/src/data/mockData.js.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend-only prototype. No backend. Popovers use a full-viewport invisible backdrop (z-30) beneath the popover panel (z-40) — clicking a different metric card while a popover is open dismisses the current popover but does not open the new one; you must click the close button (or click again) first. All required data-testids exist and work: metric-fact-check, metric-downstream-impact, metric-track-record, fact-check-popover(+ -close), downstream-impact-popover(+ -close), track-record-popover(+ -close), view-btn-{id}, details-panel, details-panel-close, details-panel-backdrop. Note that 'CHECK SEQUENCE · RUN IN ORDER' is rendered as 'Check sequence · run in order' with CSS uppercase — use case-insensitive matching.
